judgment.summary: The business maintains strong margins and cash flow despite a significant recent contraction in earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 2.70 beat the 2.68 estimate; next earnings date is 2026-07-15.
positives: Forward P/E 18.16 vs trailing 26.71 indicates a more attractive valuation profile.
Profit margin 21.8% and ROE 26.4% reflect strong profitability and capital efficiency.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $12.51B shows robust cash generation.
Revenue growth of 9.9% shows steady top-line expansion.
negatives: Earnings growth of -52.9% indicates a sharp decline in profitability.
PEG ratio of 2.91 suggests the stock is priced high relative to its growth.
Quick ratio of 0.69 indicates potential short-term liquidity constraints.
Total debt of $54.99B is significantly higher than total cash of $22.05B.

news.paragraph: Johnson & Johnson has seen a 3.5% decline since its last earnings report, despite recent reports of a Wall Street boost driven by blockbuster drug growth hopes. Options traders should consider this recent price weakness alongside positive sentiment regarding the company's valuation and its role in addressing unmet needs in the bladder cancer and mental health markets.
